name: AND Gate
description: Build a basic AND gate: output ON only when both inputs are ON.
objectives: Connect 2 lever inputs to 1 lamp output
Lamp only lights when both levers are ON
hints: Place redstone dust between both levers
A redstone torch acts as a NOT gate
AND gates need both signals reaching the lamp

name: OR Gate
description: Build an OR gate: output ON when either input is ON.
objectives: Lamp lights when either lever is ON
hints: Simply connect both levers to the same redstone line
OR is the simplest gate — no torches needed

name: NOT Gate
description: Build a NOT gate (inverter): output ON when input is OFF.
objectives: Lamp is OFF when lever is ON and vice versa
hints: Place a redstone torch adjacent to the input line
The torch inverts the signal

name: XOR Gate
description: Build an XOR gate: output ON when inputs differ.
objectives: Lamp ON when exactly one lever is ON
hints: Combine AND and OR gates with a NOT gate
Think of it as: (A OR B) AND NOT (A AND B)

name: NAND Gate
description: Build a NAND gate: NOT AND — output OFF only when both inputs are ON.
objectives: Lamp OFF only when both levers are ON
hints: Build an AND gate, then invert the output
A single redstone torch after an AND gate creates NAND

name: T Flip-Flop
description: Build a T flip-flop: each button press toggles the output state.
hints: Use a sticky piston to push/pull a redstone block
The block position represents stored state

name: RS-NOR Latch
description: Build a reset-set NOR latch with SET and RESET buttons.
objectives: SET turns lamp ON, RESET turns it OFF
hints: Cross-connect two NOR gates
Each NOR gate uses a redstone torch

name: 1Hz Clock
description: Build a clock circuit that pulses approximately once per second.
objectives: Clock output connected to a lamp
Lamp toggles roughly once per second
hints: A comparator in subtract mode with a full container makes a clock
Or use 2 repeaters facing each other in a loop

name: Pulse Shortener
description: Convert a long signal into a short pulse.
objectives: Lamp flashes briefly then turns OFF even while lever stays ON
hints: Use a piston to momentarily break a circuit
The piston retracts after a single tick

name: Item Transport Pipe
description: Build a basic item transport system using hoppers.
objectives: Items placed in a chest arrive at a destination chest
hints: Chain hoppers together pointing in the direction of travel
Use a comparator to detect when items arrive

name: Single Piston Door
description: Build a door that opens/closes with a button press.
objectives: Button extends piston, pressing again retracts
hints: Connect button directly to piston with redstone dust
Add a repeater for longer signal travel

name: 2x2 Piston Door
description: Build a hidden 2x2 piston door that opens seamlessly.
objectives: 4 blocks retract to reveal a 2x2 opening
hints: Use 4 sticky pistons (2 top, 2 bottom)
Top pistons push down, bottom push up — or use an ABBA pattern

name: Dispenser Trap
description: Build a trap that fires when a player walks on a pressure plate.
hints: Connect pressure plate directly to dispenser
Add repeaters to control timing

name: Automatic Wheat Farm
description: Build an automated wheat farm using observers and dispensers.
objectives: Observer detects growth and triggers harvest
Items are collected into a chest
hints: Place observers facing the crops
Use water to flush harvested items to hoppers

name: 1-Bit Binary Adder
description: Build a full adder circuit: adds two 1-bit numbers plus carry.
objectives: 3 inputs (A, B, Carry In), 2 outputs (Sum, Carry Out)
hints: Sum = A XOR B XOR Cin
Carry Out = (A AND B) OR (Cin AND (A XOR B))

name: Signal Strength Chain
description: Demonstrate redstone signal strength degradation over distance.
objectives: Create a line of 15+ redstone dust
Show that signal weakens at the end
Use a repeater to restore full strength
hints: Place redstone dust in a straight line from lever
Lamp at distance 15 won't light — add a repeater to fix it

name: Repeater Delay Chain
description: Use 5 repeaters to create a 10-tick delayed signal.
objectives: 5 repeaters in series, each set to 2 ticks
Signal reaches lamp 10 ticks after button press
hints: Right-click each repeater twice to set to 2-tick delay
5 repeaters × 2 ticks = 10 total ticks

name: Observer Clock
description: Build a fast clock using two observers facing each other.
objectives: 2 observers facing each other update a block
hints: Place observers facing each other with a block between them
Attach redstone dust to one observer's output face

name: 3x3 Secret Door
description: Build a seamless 3x3 hidden piston door.
objectives: 3x3 wall section retracts to reveal opening
Floor and ceiling are hidden seamlessly
hints: Use an upper and lower section with staggered timing
Slime blocks help move adjacent blocks together

name: Note Block Jukebox
description: Build a simple melody using note blocks and redstone clock.
objectives: Clock circuit triggers notes in rhythm
hints: Set note block pitches by right-clicking
Use different repeater delays for rhythm variation

name: Multi-Item Sorter
description: Build an item sorter that routes 3+ different items to different chests.
objectives: Each type goes to its own chest
hints: Use item frames with 1 item as filters
Comparators detect when hopper has 1 of the filter item

name: 4-Bit Shift Register
description: Build a 4-bit shift register using D flip-flops.
objectives: Each clock pulse shifts all bits one position
hints: Chain 4 T flip-flops together
Connect each output to the next input

name: Redstone Calculator
description: Build a working 2-bit calculator that adds two numbers (0-3) and displays the result.
objectives: Display result on lamps (up to 4 bits)
hints: Use two 1-bit adders chained together
The carry output of the first becomes the carry input of the second

name: NOR Gate
description: Build a NOR gate: output ON only when both inputs are OFF.
objectives: Lamp ON only when both levers are OFF
hints: A redstone torch naturally acts as a NOR gate
Two inputs feeding a torch = NOR

name: XNOR Gate
description: Build an XNOR gate: output ON when inputs are the same.
objectives: Lamp ON when both levers match
hints: XNOR is the opposite of XOR
Build an XOR gate and invert the output

name: Monostable Circuit
description: Build a monostable circuit that outputs a fixed-length pulse.
objectives: Button input produces a 2-tick pulse output
Output returns to OFF after pulse regardless of input
hints: Use a sticky piston to break and restore a circuit
The pulse length is controlled by repeater delay

name: Double Piston Extender
description: Build a double piston extender that extends 2 blocks.
objectives: Single button activates both in sequence
hints: The rear piston pushes the front piston + block
Timing is key — delay the front piston by 1 tick

name: Room Lighting System
description: Wire a room with lights controlled by a single lever.
hints: Run redstone under the floor
Use repeaters if the line is longer than 15 blocks

name: Auto-Smoker System
description: Build an automatic cooking system with hoppers and comparators.
objectives: Raw food goes in, cooked food comes out
System detects when fuel is low
Cooked items collect in output chest
hints: Hopper feeds items into smoker, another extracts
Comparator on fuel hopper can signal when empty

name: Combination Lock
description: Build a 3-lever combination lock that opens a door with the correct code.
objectives: Only one specific combination opens the door
hints: Use AND gates to check each lever position
Only the correct AND combination activates the door piston

name: Vertical Redstone
description: Transmit a redstone signal straight up or down 5 blocks.
objectives: Lever at bottom, lamp at top (5 blocks up)
hints: Alternating torch and block creates vertical signal
Each torch-block pair raises the signal one block

name: Automatic Night Lights
description: Build lamps that turn on automatically at night using a daylight sensor.
objectives: Lamps ON at night, OFF during day
Inverted sensor mode for night activation
hints: Right-click the daylight sensor to invert it
Inverted mode outputs signal at night

name: Minecart Launch Station
description: Build a minecart station that launches carts on a button press.
hints: Detector rail at the end sends signal back
Use a T flip-flop for toggle launch

name: Binary to Decimal Display
description: Build a 4-bit binary to decimal display (0-15) using lamps.
objectives: Display the correct decimal value on lamps
hints: Build a decoder for each decimal digit
Each output lamp needs its own combinational logic
